name: Salmon with Creamy Horseradish Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, combine the white wine, chopped onion, lemon juice, and peppercorns. Cover and cook over medium-high heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
2. Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the salmon steaks; cover and simmer until the salmon flakes easily when tested with a fork (about 5-6 minutes, depending on thickness).
3. While cooking the salmon, prepare the horseradish sauce in a small mixing bowl. Combine the sour cream, horseradish, flour, chopped mint, and white pepper; stir to combine and set aside.
4. Transfer the cooked salmon to a serving platter and keep warm.
5. Pour the cooking liquid through a sieve into a bowl, discarding any solids. Return the sauce to the skillet and cook over medium-high heat until it comes to a boil. Stir in the horseradish sauce. Reduce the heat to low and simmer, stirring frequently, until the mixture thickens (about 2-3 minutes).
6. Pour the sauce over the salmon.
